rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AILEEN DANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: An appearance is required. The Court is to determine whether the conservatee's parents, as the obligors under Family Code § 3910(a), may be "ordered" to pay the petitioner's fees and her attorneys' fees and costs to discharge their duty of support owed to the adult conservatee/child in this proceeding. ****NOTE: MEEING ID AND PASSWORD HAVE BEEN UPDATED BELOW**** As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. New Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 New Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANK BRUNO LEONARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 5th amended general order re the strong preference for remote appearances. as a result of the covid-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th amended general order) or by videoconference using Zoom. to appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Webinar ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 to appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the Webinar ID and password above. Examiner Notes: 1) Attorney appears to have filed another Petition for Final Distribution without any attempt at complying with the Local Rules, CRC, and Probate Code. Examiner is concerned counsel is unable to properly close the estate and has attached a list of paralegals. 2) The accounting does not comply with the Local Rules or Probate Code and has $0 for all entries. The 03/20/19 Final I&A has assets of $1,250,000.00. $200,000.00 in cash and 50% interest in 167-169 States Street San Francisco. 3) The proposed distribution does not conform with Decedent's Will and states should be distributed by the terms of the decedent's will/Pursuant to the Jaws of intestate succession. 4) Calculation of attorney fees does not follow CRC 7.705. 5) The US Bank CD statement has a maturity date of 12/4/18 and what happened to the money is not explained in the accounting.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN JOSEPH NOONAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/18/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proof of service of notice of the hearing on the subject petition is on file. 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Paragraph 9 of the petition alleges that the decedent received no Medi-Cal benefits. Why did the petitioner pay $1,802 to the Department of Health Care Services? B) Paragraph 12 must state all information required by CRC 7.403. C) Paragraph 15 must state all information required by CRC 7.250. D) Paragraph 16 is silent as to real property taxes. E) What did the petitioner mean by "this compensation" as so alleged in paragraph 21 of the petition? F) A detailed schedule of losses on sale must be filed in support of the allegation in paragraph 23 of the petition. CRC 7.550(b)(6). G) Paragraph 25 of the petition must show specific descriptions of the assets on hand. CRC 7.651. H) While paragraph 6 of the proposed order states that unknown or newly discovered property must be distributed to the heirs in equal shares, there is no corresponding allegation or prayer for relief in the petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLEN YOSHIO YASAKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/11/2020. The following issues remain unresolved: 1) According to the declaration (filed 07/20/2020), the decedent's mother returned $125,000 to the petitioner, but her creditor's claim has not been withdrawn, nor is the petitioner's rejection of the claim on file. See also CRC 7.403(5) & (6). 2) Did the petitioner give notice of proposed action before donating the Honda Civic? See Probate Code §§ 10510 & 10513. 3) How did the petitioner acquire the shares of each security set forth in paragraph 17(r)-(ff)?

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA CO.** IN THE MATTER OF THE AMARO LALAND ROMERO MINOR'S SETTLEMENT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds, And Changing Venue And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/01/2020: The following issues must be addressed: 1) Adding requests for appointment of Herb Thomas as trustee and periodic payments of his trustee compensation on account is an amendment, rather than a supplement, but neither requests were included in the caption of the document titled "supplement" (filed 06/26/2020) or notice of the hearing (filed 06/30/2020). See CRC 7.3(4), 7.50, 7.53(a) & 7.102. An amendment to the petition and proof of service of notice of the amendment specifically referring to the trustee appointment and compensation on account must be given. 2) A proposed hourly fee schedule or statement of compensation must be filed by Herb Thomas. Probate Code § 2643.1(a). 3) Notice of how to file an objection to the proposed trustee's compensation on account must also be given in accordance with § 2643.1(c)(1).

Webinar ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 to appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the Webinar ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALD EDWARDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: An appearance is required. Counsel is to address the following issues for the Court: 1) The proof of publication (filed 07/08/2020) states that an AMENDED petition for probate has been filed, but the petition does not appear to have been amended. 2) As the nominating sole heir is a non-resident of the United States, the factors set forth in Probate Code § 8465(d)(1)-(4) must be addressed. ****NOTE: MEEING ID AND PASSWORD HAVE BEEN UPDATED BELOW**** As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. New Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 New Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.
